old_claim.note: Optical Workshops provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Earlier glass and crystal lenses are the material/craft lineage for later spectacle-lens grinding, but not the broader workshop institution itself.

source_shape.source_locator: Ilardi, chapter six, pp. 224-226, links better lenses to clear glass or crystal properly ground and polished and discusses advances in hand tools for grinding and polishing lenses.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source ties spectacle-lens workmanship to glass/crystal lens material and grinding/polishing practice.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old optical_workshops edge treated the broader workshop category as a prerequisite of one of its own lens-grinding specializations; glass_lenses_early preserves the older material/craft lineage without making the parent workshop node a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Lens Grinding Workshops depended on Optical Workshops, a broader neighboring node that already includes grinding lenses and assembling optical instruments.
ontology_after: Lens Grinding Workshops depends on earlier glass/crystal lens craft plus spectacles and guild/shop context; Optical Workshops is no longer a direct prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: optical_workshops is removed and replaced with glass_lenses_early as a historical predecessor.
would_reject_if: The optical_workshops node were narrowed to a strictly earlier institution that did not already include lens grinding.
A stronger source showed a separate broad optical-workshop institution necessarily preceded specialized lens-grinding shops.
The lens_grinding_workshops node were merged into optical_workshops as a duplicate rather than kept as a specific enabling craft node for telescope and fitting practices.
short_reason: Glass/crystal lens craft is the direct predecessor; optical_workshops was the broader parent concept.
